edible_parts: Ripe blue-black fruits (drupes); each holds a single flat seed with a thin layer of sweet pulp.
other_uses: Excellent wildlife hedge and ornamental; dense growth gives nesting cover and the fall fruit feeds many birds and mammals.
prep: Eat fully ripe fruit out of hand — sweet with a date- or prune-like flavor. Also cooked into jams, sauces, and fruit leathers; sieve out the large seeds. Flavor improves as fruit softens on the plant.
harvest: Late summer through fall (Sept–Oct); sweeter after the first frosts.
soil: Adaptable; prefers moist, well-drained soils but tolerates a range including limestone and clay.
risks: Ripe fruit is safe and edible; the large single seed and thin pulp are the main limitation. No significant toxicity reported.
buy: Widely available from native-plant nurseries (e.g. Prairie Moon Nursery, Prairie Nursery) as bare-root or potted stock.
